meta.pageTitle: Timothy J. Owen — AI Product Support

header.name: Dr. Timothy J. Owen
header.subtitle: U.S. Army Veteran · AI Product Support · South Puget Sound, WA

objective: Washington Vets-to-Tech graduate and retired 20-year U.S. Army veteran with hands-on experience supporting government IT users, active **CompTIA Network+** and **AWS Cloud Practitioner** certifications, and extensive practical fluency with large language models across Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, and Perplexity. Exceptional written and verbal communicator with a career-long track record of translating complex technical and conceptual material for non-expert audiences. Brings deep user empathy, rigorous documentation discipline, and genuine curiosity about AI systems to every support interaction. Active **federal Secret-level security clearance**.

transferableSkills.title: LLM Fluency
transferableSkills.description: Daily practical use of Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, and Perplexity across complex projects — including multi-model design councils, architectural specification, and algorithmic problem-solving. I understand how these models work in practice: where they excel, where they struggle, and how to help a user get from confusion to resolution.

transferableSkills.title: Knowledge Transfer at Scale
transferableSkills.description: Facilitated certification of 200 Master Resilience Trainers; contributed to curriculum development that reduced training time by 58% across 5,000+ personnel. A career built on making complex material accessible to non-expert audiences.

transferableSkills.title: Security & Compliance
transferableSkills.description: Active federal Secret-level security clearance. Completed WaV2T Cybersecurity program covering authentication systems, HIPAA compliance, and security hardening — directly applicable to enterprise and regulated-sector customer support.

transferableSkills.title: Ownership Under Pressure
transferableSkills.description: 20 years maintaining 100% operational readiness in mission-critical environments — including on-call and overnight rotations. Brings the initiative and accountability to manage high-urgency user issues with extreme ownership.

bullets: Provided first-line technical support to government agency staff, logging and tracking service requests, communicating technical requirements clearly to non-technical users, and escalating complex cases with detailed documentation
Supported account management, software configuration, and workstation setup — direct experience in government and public sector support
Operated within strict data privacy policies and compliance requirements, developing security and compliance discipline relevant to enterprise customer support

Completed 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science; 100% lab completion rate across security hardening, threat intelligence, vulnerability assessment, and cloud technologies
Developed working knowledge of authentication systems and security compliance frameworks applicable to enterprise SSO troubleshooting and regulated-sector customer support
Completed 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science; 100% lab completion rate across Linux, Windows Server, networking, AWS, and Python scripting
Developed Python automation scripts for support workflows and completed hands-on networking labs covering TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, and VPN configuration

Communicated complex technical and regulatory requirements to personnel at every level of a large organization — from senior leaders to entry-level staff — in high-pressure, time-sensitive environments

Facilitated training and certification of 200 Master Resilience Trainers as a Level 2 Facilitator across 10 cohorts, developing structured knowledge transfer and adult learning skills that underpin effective technical upleveling
Managed onboarding and offboarding of 40+ personnel, including account access and equipment assignment, with rigorous documentation throughout
Administered federal security clearance compliance for 40+ personnel with meticulous records management

Instructed 100+ students and 20 mid-level leaders to a 100% pass rate — including several at-risk students — through individualized coaching demonstrating persistent user empathy and ownership-driven problem-solving
Advised 40 senior leaders as subject-matter expert; assessed 400+ student performances; maintained records for 80 personnel
Contributed to curriculum and policy development for 5,000+ personnel, resulting in a 58% reduction in training cycle time through clear, reusable documentation
Earned Distinguished Leader Award, Army Leadership Development Program (2010)
Managed HR records and asset documentation for 40+ personnel across international assignments
Deployed in support of Operation Iraqi Freedom (2007); earned promotion to Sergeant in 2008

education.degree: Washington Vets-to-Tech Cybersecurity Certificate (18 credits, B.S. Computer Science)
education.note: CSC 325 Computer Security · CSC 364 Cybersecurity Threat Intelligence · CSC 426 Vulnerability Assessment · CSC 455 Cloud Technologies

education.degree: Washington Vets-to-Tech Server & Cloud Applications Certificate (18 credits, B.S. Computer Science)
education.note: CSC 160, 330, 395, 331, 235, 456 — Networking, Linux, Windows Server, Cloud
Dissertation: "Integrating the Music Learning Theory of Edwin E. Gordon with a Beginning Saxophone Curriculum"

projects.title: SIP — Systematic Instrumental Practice
projects.description: Dependency-aware spaced repetition scheduling application for serious instrumental musicians. Uses FSRS-5 scheduling, Flutter (Android-first), Python/FastAPI backend, and py-fsrs. Grounded in doctoral dissertation research applying Edwin Gordon's Music Learning Theory to systematic skill acquisition.

projects.title: Multi-Model AI Design Council
projects.description: Conducted structured design reviews of SIP's architecture using Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, and Perplexity simultaneously — producing a canonical design specification and developing deep comparative understanding of each model's strengths, reasoning patterns, and limitations.

projects.title: Python Automation Tools
projects.description: Subnet Calculator, VA Ratings Calculator, and Tempo Finder (binary-search algorithm) — demonstrating scripting, problem-solving, and algorithmic thinking transferable to support automation and tooling.
